seo.title: RAG Retrieval Quality Optimization in Haystack Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix rag retrieval quality optimization in Haystack agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Haystack pipelines, RAG Retrieval Quality Optimization issues manifest as retrievers returning irrelevant documents due to poor query embedding alignment or index quality, leading to hallucinated generations that diverge from provided context. Pipeline component mismatches between retriever and generator expected formats silently degrade output relevance, while custom dense retrievers fail without clear error surfacing. These problems cascade through composable pipelines, making production RAG systems unreliable without systematic quality checks.

howItManifests.scenario: Sparse retriever returns keyword-matched but semantically irrelevant docs
howItManifests.symptom: BM25 scores high but documents lack semantic overlap with query
howItManifests.impact: Generator fabricates answers from unrelated context, eroding trust

howItManifests.scenario: Dense retriever embedding drift from index updates
howItManifests.symptom: Cosine similarity scores drop below 0.7 threshold consistently
howItManifests.impact: Pipeline latency increases as reranking fails to filter noise

howItManifests.scenario: Document store schema changes break retriever output format
howItManifests.symptom: Generator receives NoneType instead of Document objects
howItManifests.impact: Silent failures produce empty or malformed responses

howItManifests.scenario: Custom embedding model returns inconsistent vector dimensions
howItManifests.symptom: Pipeline validation passes but retrieval scores are NaN
howItManifests.impact: All retrieved documents score identically, breaking ranking

detection.name: Retriever Relevance Score Monitoring
detection.description: Extracts cosine similarity scores from DensePassageRetriever and flags pipelines where >30% of documents fall below 0.6 threshold
detection.code:
from haystack import Pipeline
from haystack.components.retrievers import InMemoryEmbeddingRetriever
import numpy as np

def check_retrieval_quality(pipeline: Pipeline, test_queries: list[str], threshold: float = 0.6) -> dict:
    results = []
    for query in test_queries:
        output = pipeline.run(query=query)
        docs = output.get('documents', [])
        scores = [doc.meta.get('score', 0) for doc in docs[:5]]
        low_relevance = sum(1 for s in scores if s < threshold) / max(len(scores), 1)
        results.append({'query': query, 'low_relevance_ratio': low_relevance})
    avg_degradation = np.mean([r['low_relevance_ratio'] for r in results])
    return {'degraded': avg_degradation > 0.3, 'metric': avg_degradation}

detection.name: Document Context Overlap Check
detection.description: Measures semantic overlap between query and top-3 retrieved documents using sentence transformers
detection.code:
from sentence_transformers import SentenceTransformer, util
import numpy as np

def measure_context_overlap(query: str, documents: list, model_name='all-MiniLM-L6-v2') -> float:
    model = SentenceTransformer(model_name)
    query_emb = model.encode(query)
    doc_texts = [doc.content for doc in documents[:3]]
    doc_embs = model.encode(doc_texts)
    similarities = util.cos_sim(query_emb, doc_embs)[0]
    return float(np.mean(similarities))

detection.name: Pipeline Type Safety Validator
detection.description: Verifies retriever outputs Document objects expected by downstream rankers/generators

fixes.name: Hybrid Retriever with Reranking
fixes.description: Combines BM25 sparse + dense retrieval with cross-encoder reranking for improved precision
fixes.code:
from haystack.components.retrievers import BM25Retriever, EmbeddingRetriever
from haystack.components.rankers import SentenceTransformersCrossEncoder

hybrid_pipe = Pipeline()
hybrid_pipe.add_component("sparse", BM25Retriever())
hybrid_pipe.add_component("dense", EmbeddingRetriever())
hybrid_pipe.add_component("reranker", SentenceTransformersCrossEncoder(model="cross-encoder/ms-marco-MiniLM-L-6-v2"))
hybrid_pipe.connect("sparse.documents", "reranker.documents")
hybrid_pipe.connect("dense.documents", "reranker.documents")

fixes.name: Dynamic Embedding Model Switching
fixes.description: Fallback to domain-specific embeddings when default retriever degrades
fixes.code:
from haystack.components.embedders import SentenceTransformersDocumentEmbedder, SentenceTransformersTextEmbedder

# Primary embedding
primary_embedder = SentenceTransformersTextEmbedder(model="all-MiniLM-L6-v2")

# Fallback for technical domains
fallback_embedder = SentenceTransformersTextEmbedder(model="sentence-transformers/all-mpnet-base-v2")

retriever = EmbeddingRetriever(
    document_store=doc_store,
    embedding_model=primary_embedder,
    fallback_embedding_model=fallback_embedder
)

fixes.name: Context-Aware Generator Guardrails
fixes.description: Forces generator to cite retrieved context or admit knowledge gaps
fixes.code:
from haystack.components.generators import OpenAIGenerator

generator = OpenAIGenerator(
    model="gpt-4",
    system_prompt="""You are a helpful assistant. ONLY answer based on the provided context.
If the context doesn't contain the answer, say 'I don't have sufficient context to answer this.'
Always cite specific passages from the context using [1], [2] etc.""",
    generation_kwargs={"temperature": 0.1, "max_tokens": 512}
)

vexAutomation.heading: Automating RAG Retrieval Quality Optimization Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates as a custom Haystack component that continuously monitors retrieval quality across all pipeline runs, automatically detecting embedding drift, relevance degradation, and component mismatches. It maintains rolling baselines of cosine similarity scores and context overlap metrics, flagging pipelines when performance drops >15% from golden datasets. Vex's agentic remediation automatically swaps embedding models, adjusts retriever thresholds, or injects reranking components without pipeline redeployment.

The VexGuard component wraps existing retrievers, extracting metrics at runtime and triggering automated fixes through Haystack's dynamic pipeline reconfiguration. Production teams gain observability into RAG health with zero instrumentation overhead, while Vex's ML-based anomaly detection catches subtle quality regressions before they impact end users. Integration takes <5 minutes via pip install and pipeline.add_component().
vexAutomation.code:
from vex_sdk.haystack import VexGuard
from haystack import Pipeline

# Install: pip install haystack-ai vex-sdk
rag_pipeline = Pipeline()

# Add Vex as first component for full observability
rag_pipeline.add_component("vex_guard", VexGuard(monitor=['retrieval_quality', 'context_overlap']))
rag_pipeline.add_component("retriever", EmbeddingRetriever(document_store=doc_store))
rag_pipeline.add_component("generator", OpenAIGenerator(model="gpt-4"))

rag_pipeline.connect("vex_guard.documents", "retriever.query")
rag_pipeline.connect("retriever.documents", "generator.documents")

# Vex auto-generates eval datasets and baselines
result = rag_pipeline.run(query="What is RAG?", vex_evaluate=True)

# Access automated insights
print(result['vex_guard']['quality_score'])  # 0.87
print(result['vex_guard']['recommended_fix'])  # 'Add reranker component'

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Add one line of code to monitor and auto-fix RAG quality issues in production Haystack pipelines today.
